name: Acceleration (Overview)
description: Change in velocity—how fast something speeds up, slows down, or changes direction

content.overview: Acceleration answers: **IS THE VELOCITY CHANGING?** A car speeding up, a ball slowing down, a car turning—all acceleration. Acceleration is ANY change in velocity.
content.explanation:
Acceleration = change in velocity ÷ time

Acceleration is a VECTOR—it has magnitude and direction.

**Three types of acceleration:**

1. **Speeding up:** Stepping on the gas. Acceleration forward.
2. **Slowing down:** Braking. Acceleration backward (sometimes called deceleration).
3. **Changing direction:** Turning a corner at constant speed. Still acceleration!

**Example:**
- Car goes 0 to 60 m/s in 10 seconds
- Acceleration = (60 - 0) m/s ÷ 10 s = 6 m/s²

**Common mistake:**
People think acceleration = speeding up. WRONG. A car going 100 km/h turning a corner IS accelerating (direction changes). A car braking IS accelerating (slowing down). Any velocity change = acceleration.

**Instantaneous vs average:**
- Average acceleration = (final velocity - initial velocity) ÷ time
- Instantaneous acceleration = acceleration at one moment

Acceleration can be negative (if velocity decreases or direction reverses).

**Units:**
- m/s² (meters per second squared) - SI unit
- This will make more sense once you see how acceleration works in equations
keyPoints: Speeding up, slowing down, changing direction—all acceleration
Caused by forces (Newton's laws coming next)
content.whyItMatters: Acceleration is what forces create. Understanding acceleration bridges kinematics (motion) to dynamics (forces). This is the bridge to the rest of mechanics.
